citations.narrative:
One side of the primary enclosures housing African crested porcupines is positioned directly against the perimeter fence.
This means that there is not at least three feet between the edge of the enclosure and the perimeter fence.
Primary enclosures that are less than three feet away from the perimeter fence may allow unwanted contact between
animals inside the enclosure and people or animals outside the perimeter fence which could lead to injury or disease
spread. All perimeter fences must be of sufficient height (at least six feet high) to keep animals and unauthorized persons
out. It must be of sufficient distance (at least three feet) from the outside of the primary enclosure to prevent physical
contact between animals inside the enclosure and animals or persons outside the perimeter fence.
To be corrected by March 16, 2026.

In the indoor portion of the enclosure housing two sloths, there is an accumulation of rodent droppings. There are
droppings on the mat covering the floor as well as on the platforms that hold their food and water bowl.
Pests, such as rodents, can spread illness and disease to the animals and contaminate an animal's living environment. A
safe and effective program for the control of insects, ectoparasites, and avian and mammalian pests shall be established
and maintained.
To be corrected by February 23, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.127(d) 
   
Facilities, outdoor. 
One side of the primary enclosures housing African crested porcupines is positioned directly against the perimeter fence. 
This means that there is not at least three feet between the edge of the enclosure and the perimeter fence. 
Primary enclosures that are less than three feet away from the perimeter fence may allow unwanted contact between 
animals inside the enclosure and people or animals outside the perimeter fence which could lead to injury or disease 
spread. All perimeter fences must be of sufficient height (at least six feet high) to keep animals and unauthorized persons 
out. It must be of sufficient distance (at least three feet) from the outside of the primary enclosure to prevent physical 
contact between animals inside the enclosure and animals or persons outside the perimeter fence. 
To be corrected by March 16, 2026. 
3.131(d) 
   
Sanitation. 
In the indoor portion of the enclosure housing two sloths, there is an accumulation of rodent droppings. There are 
droppings on the mat covering the floor as well as on the platforms that hold their food and water bowl.  
Pests, such as rodents, can spread illness and disease to the animals and contaminate an animal's living environment. A 
safe and effective program for the control of insects, ectoparasites, and avian and mammalian pests shall be established 
and maintained.  
To be corrected by February 23, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
